title: Another Epitaph
content:
This little vault, this narrow room,
    Of Love and Beauty is the tomb;
    The dawning beam, that �gan to clear
    Our clouded sky, lies darken�d here,
    For ever set to us: by Death
    Sent to enflame the World Beneath.
    �Twas but a bud, yet did contain
    More sweetness than shall spring again;
    A budding Star, that might have grown
    Into a Sun when it had blown.
    This hopeful Beauty did create
    New life in Love�s declining state;
    But now his empire ends, and we
    From fire and wounding darts are free;
    His brand, his bow, let no man fear:
    The flames, the arrows, all lie here.
meaning:
Thomas Carew's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Another Epitaph"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
